document.purpose: Rules and patterns cheat sheet for AI/agent preflight. Read first, then task-specific docs.

doc_guide.01_ARCHITECTURE.md: System design, data model, storage, invariants, delete/version policy, AI control
doc_guide.02_WORKFLOWS.md: Upload, chat, delete, session cleanup workflows with diagrams
doc_guide.03_API_CONTRACTS.md: Full route reference, security, rate limits, error handling
doc_guide.04_DEPLOYMENT.md: Docker topology, nginx, env vars, health probes, backup
doc_guide.05_INGESTION_RETRIEVAL.md: 13-step pipeline, PaddleOCR (mandatory), embedding specs, retrieval deep dive

core_invariants.name: async_ingestion
core_invariants.rule: Upload returns task_id immediately. Parsing/indexing runs asynchronously via Celery workers.

core_invariants.name: four_stage_retrieval
core_invariants.rule: Hybrid search (dense + BM25 RRF fusion) → section grouping (≥0.30) → dedup → rerank (optional, off by default) → full section context assembly. Retrieve at chunk level, present full section content to LLM. Multi-query expansion opt-in. Doc IDs TTL-cached 60s.

core_invariants.name: hard_delete_order
core_invariants.rule: 6 steps: registry → vectors → sections → file → DB row → purge. Sections before DB row (referential integrity).

core_invariants.name: api_gateway_proxy
core_invariants.rule: Browser never sees Bearer token. All calls via /api/bep/ → Next.js Route Handler → getToken() → backend.

data_location_map.notes: ~400 token chunks with section_id metadata. Named dense + sparse-bm25 vectors.
data/bm25_vocab.json — rebuilt on upload/delete. TTL cache 120s. Underthesea tokenization + IDF.
DB persisted (tokens_in, tokens_out, latency_ms, model_used); Redis hot cache (pipeline atomic). hydrate_from_db() on expiry.
Hard-deleted after 30 days by Celery Beat. Sidebar ordered by updated_at DESC.
Redis cache 5min TTL; injected into AI systemInstruction
Token revocation on logout. Role cached in JWT payload.

ai.AI_OUTPUT_COST_PER_1M: 0.0 (set to actual cost when switching models)

anti_patterns: direct provider SDK calls in routes
direct raise HTTPException in API layer (use http_errors helpers)
SessionLocal or SQLAlchemy Session in route files (use Repository via Service)
business logic in route handlers (belongs in Service layer)

architecture_layers.description: Strict 3-layer separation. Routes = HTTP only. Services = business logic. Repositories = data access.

controller.responsibility: HTTP parsing, auth deps, response formatting, status codes. NO business logic, NO SessionLocal, NO direct DB queries.

files.chat/chat_service.py: ChatService — prepare_chat (full orchestration), session mgmt, message persistence, extract_memories
files.chat/memory_service.py: MemoryService — CRUD for user memories (receives UserMemoryService via DI)
files.chat/user_memory_service.py: UserMemoryService — Redis + MemoryRepository via DI (no SessionLocal)
files.documents/document_service.py: DocumentService — upload, status, delete, retry (uses build_vector_store factory)
files.documents/cleanup_service.py: CleanupService — hard delete orchestration (class, uses DocumentRepository + SectionRepository)
files.ingestion/ingestion_service.py: IngestionService — accepts optional section_repo via constructor
files.ingestion/recovery_service.py: RecoveryService — uses Repository methods instead of raw ORM
files.retrieval/retrieval_service.py: retrieve_context(), build_answer() — uses DocumentRepository + SectionRepository methods
files.retrieval/query_expand.py: expand_query() — multi-query domain logic for RAG

repository.convention: {domain}_repository.py naming. Takes Session via constructor, returns dicts (not ORM models)

di_wiring.pattern: Repository factories yield from SessionLocal, Service factories take Repository via Depends()

priority: Preserve public APIs and route contracts
Prefer existing service layers and adapters
do: Read AGENTS.md first, then 00_QUICK_REFERENCE, then task-specific docs
Reuse existing types, constants, and helpers
Use nh3 for HTML sanitization, PyJWT for JWT
do_not: Invent new architecture unless docs require it
Rewrite unrelated files during small fixes
Change endpoint names or response fields silently
Return confident answers without grounded evidence

infrastructure.hardware_auto_detect: app/core/hardware.py — 3-tier: TIGHT GPU (VRAM headroom<6GB→1 uvicorn worker), COMFORTABLE GPU (VRAM headroom≥6GB→multi-worker), CPU (min(cpu, ram//2, 8)). VRAM headroom = total VRAM − 2GB (embedding+reranker). Butler uses celery multi: node-ingestion (solo, GPU) + node-default (prefork, CPU, Beat)
infrastructure.redis_config: maxmemory via REDIS_MAXMEMORY env var (default 512mb for dev), allkeys-lru, save 60 1000, appendonly yes
infrastructure.redis_db_separation: DB 0: Celery broker, DB 1: result backend, DB 2: app cache
infrastructure.celery_config: All values configurable via env vars: time limits, memory limit, visibility timeout, retry, max-tasks-per-child. Butler uses celery multi with 2 nodes: node-ingestion (solo, GPU, ingestion queue) + node-default (prefork, CPU, cleanup+default queues, Beat scheduler). Routing configured in ops/entrypoint-worker.sh

ingestion_queue: rebuild_bm25_index_task — BM25 vocab rebuild from Qdrant after upload/delete (maintenance_tasks.py)
cleanup_queue: cleanup_old_chat_sessions_task — session TTL cleanup (cleanup_pipeline.py, Beat daily)
cleanup_orphaned_vectors_task — remove Qdrant vectors without DB sections (maintenance_tasks.py, Beat daily)
default_queue: extract_memories_task — async memory extraction, replaces asyncio.create_task (memory_tasks.py)

infrastructure.api_resource_limits: 4 CPUs, 6G RAM (Docker deploy limits) — increase for production
